content: The human linguistic concept of reflection as both a physical act of bouncing light and a metacognitive act of self-examination can inform the design of more grounded LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where we can explicitly anchor LLM self-reflection to 'bouncing' off inconsistent external outputs the same way light bounces off a surface to reveal an object's shape. This maps directly to the recent Self-Contrast approach that leverages inconsistent solving perspectives to improve reflection.
